picks.reason: Luzardo's full-sample profile (3.23 xFIP, 20.3% K-BB%) is genuinely elite and outclasses Phillips' [small sample] arm, but PHI at -175 implies ~64% and my number lands close to that given MIA's strong 126 wRC+ vs LHP offsetting. Phillips small sample also caps any conviction. Gap under 4 pts.
Toronto starter is TBD — automatic pass per data integrity rules. Cannot estimate edge without confirmed pitcher data.

Griffin's AGG profile (3.82 xFIP, 16.5 K-BB%) is solid and WAS offense is hot, but Wacha is a steady veteran (4.47 xFIP) and WAS at -134 implies ~57%. My fair sits within 3 pts. RL moved hard toward WAS already, suggesting sharp money is in.

Davis Martin's 2.41 ERA wildly outpaces his 4.09 AGG xFIP and 46.5% HH — a regression candidate, but his line is being faded since CHW is the underdog. Cole at -145 has only 22 IP [sm] this year with a weak recent xFIP; both arms uncertain. No clean edge.

Senga [sm 20 IP, 9.00 ERA] and Singer both have ugly seasonal ERAs but decent AGG xFIPs (~4.4); GABP and two mediocre arms point Over, but the Under price is stale-flagged so I treat the total market as absent. ML at NYM -123 sits near my fair given Senga's small-sample risk.

Gasser [sm, 5.85 AGG xFIP] is a genuine concern but recent L14 looks sharp; Cecconi (4.12 AGG xFIP) is the steadier arm. MIL's home offense (113 wRC+ vs RHP) and elite run differential support -154, landing my number near market. No qualifying gap.

King (4.21 AGG xFIP, 14.2 K-BB%) edges Pallante (4.20 xFIP but weak 7.9 K-BB%), and the near-pick'em ML reflects two similar clubs. SD offense vs RHP is poor (82 wRC+) though, balancing King's edge. My fair sits within the no-vig band.

Cabrera (3.70 AGG xFIP, 16.1 K-BB%, 200 IP) is meaningfully better than his ERA and dominates a weak COL road lineup (91 wRC+) backed by a dreadful Rockies bullpen. Feltner (4.54 xFIP, 9.1 K-BB%, 11.8 Brl%) is a poor arm in a spot CHC should win comfortably; -1.5 at plus-money is the value side over a -191 ML. My RL-cover estimate ~55% vs +100 implied ~50%.

Matthews (4.02 AGG xFIP) actually grades better than Rocker (4.35 xFIP, weak 9.8 K-BB%), but Rocker's home edge and TEX's pitcher-friendly park support -138. The two arms' projections roughly offset and my fair lands near market. No clean gap.

Hunter Brown's full-sample profile (3.15 xFIP, 20.7 K-BB%, 196 IP) is elite and Valdez (3.65 xFIP) is also strong — two quality arms in a low-total spot lean Under, but Brown's current-season sample is only 10.2 IP [sm] which caps a 3-unit consideration. HOU at -157 with elite Hader bullpen is fairly priced; my ML number sits near market.

Detmers' full-sample profile (3.48 xFIP, 22.1 K-BB%, 105 IP) is excellent and his recent form is genuinely supported by peripherals, while Merrill Kelly (4.21 AGG xFIP but 5.46 ERA, 6.01 SIERA L14, 15% Brl) is in real decline. AZ is favored on name/home value at near pick'em; I make LAA the modest favorite (~53%) vs -105 implied ~51%, and AZ's offense vs LHP (118) is the lone offset I've already shaved for.
picks.data_gap: Roof status (108F suggests closed); confirmed lineups.

picks.reason: Sutter Health Park (121 runs factor) plus Perkins (5.09 AGG xFIP) and Keller's collapsing L14 (6.99 xFIP) point Over the 11.0, but the Over price is stale-flagged so I treat that total market as absent. PIT ML +119 vs two shaky arms is tempting but PIT's own 2-8 L10 and Keller's recent walk explosion keep my number near market.
picks.data_gap: Live Over total price (current flagged stale).

picks.reason: Gilbert's full-sample profile (3.23 xFIP, 23.9 K-BB%, 210 IP) is one of the best arms on the slate, in a pitcher-friendly park (86 runs factor), versus Brandon Young whose 3.04 ERA badly masks a 4.58 AGG xFIP and 43.8% HH — a clear regression candidate. SEA's strong home offense vs RHP (119) adds support; I make SEA ~64% vs -154 implied ~60%.

Rasmussen's full-sample profile (3.43 xFIP, 17.8 K-BB%, 223 IP) is excellent and his peripherals are genuinely dominant, while Wrobleski's 2.95 ERA dramatically overperforms a 4.67 AGG xFIP and 9.4 K-BB% — a strong regression candidate getting market respect off ERA. TB's bullpen (Baker, Kelly) is sharp; I make TB ~50% vs +125 implied ~44%. LAD offense is elite which caps this to one unit.

parlay.reason: Both legs are independent regression-fade plays anchored on elite full-sample starters (Gilbert, Rasmussen) facing ERA-darling opponents whose peripherals point to decline (Young 4.58 xFIP, Wrobleski 4.67 xFIP). Different leagues, different parks, fully uncorrelated outcomes. My combined 32% beats the +135 implied ~42.5%... — correction: implied is ~42.5%, so I only take this at 1 unit given my 32% lands below implied; I pass the parlay as not clearing the gate.
